data.title: "The Customer has Spoken"; Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips as Ford Promises Priority Shift
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) is not blind to its own results. Electric vehicle sales dropped like a rock in 2025, and as such, Ford has no plans to repeat the mistakes of last year. Its battle plan going forward will still involve electric vehicles, but will also bring in hybrid vehicles as well. Investors seemed skeptical, though, based on the fractional drop seen in Ford shares in Thursday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

There will be some new all-electric vehicles coming, reports note, but these will be released with a focus on affordability. These are likely the models produced under the Universal EV Platform that we have heard about several times in the past. And, there will likely also be a bit more focus on the notion that an electric vehicle of some sort contains more than enough wattage to keep your home up and running during a power outage.

Ford's recent earnings report noted a 6% rise in overall sales, but a loss of around 15% in the electric vehicle division. This led CEO Jim Farley to declare, "So I think the customer has spoken. That's the punchline."

A New Mustang

One new potential prospect is already capturing some drivers' imaginations. Specifically, the idea of a new Ford Mustang that would be unlike any other Ford has released. The idea here is that there may be a Mustang sedan in the future.

Ford has been considering releasing a sedan for some time, as drivers clamor for more affordable options, and Ford itself sees sales drop on many models. Putting the Mustang name in connection with that sedan could help bridge perception gaps, and potentially, could drive interest in a car some might be hesitant to buy. The idea of a four-door sedan Mustang left some purists cold, but those purists may have been silenced unexpectedly by the need for a new, affordable car line.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 12 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 47.5% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.88 per share implies 0.76% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: The Arc Series Is Not Out of Action Yet: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slides Regardless
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) offered some good news for those who have enjoyed the Arc Battlemage graphics processing unit (GPU) so far. This particular item does not seem to be going away any time soon. A closer look at Intel's new GPU drivers suggests the Arc series will stick around for quite a while. Investors, though, were oddly displeased about this, based on the loss of over 2% that Intel shares saw in Thursday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A look at the Intel Arc 32.0.101.8509 WHQL driver package, just released earlier this week, featured an exciting conclusion that the Arc line is here to stay. In fact, the new drivers are offering benefit to older Intel Arc releases as well. The ASRock Arc A770 Phantom OC, in the 16 gigabyte variety, has had "…something of a second spring…" thanks to the addition of multi-frame generation (MFG) support.

Reports also noted that the Alchemist variant is doing well too, especially for those who use the XeLL option, which offers a low latency function. Recent tests were conducted using several different PC games, including Hogwarts Legacy and Dying Light: The Beast. While some games did better than others, there was still solid performance reported across the entire lineup of tested properties.

OpenPGL Project Gets Moved

Intel recently shut down operations for the OpenPGL project, but the project managed to find new life elsewhere. The project, which allowed for significant improvement for path-based rendering systems, found use at places like Blender. But Intel shut the project down last year, and the Academy Software Foundation (ASWF) quietly picked up the project.

A vote by the Technical Advisory Council allowed the ASWF to step in on OpenPGL, and with it, offer access to its various components once more. Reports suggest this is a good fit, as the ASWF is a hosted project of the Linux Foundation, allowing for more developments in open-source operations.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 74.24% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 8.11% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Mortgage Rates Drop to Near 4-Year Low as Pending Home Sales Tumble to Record Low
data.description:
Homes just got a bit more affordable. The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage fell to 6.01%, the lowest level since September 2022, data from Freddie Mac (FMCC) showed on Thursday. The average rate was 6.09% during the previous week and 6.85% a year ago.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Home price growth has also eased, rising by less than 2% on an annual basis over the past 10 months. "With far more homes for sale than people who want to buy them, the buyers who are in the market have the power to negotiate on price, which is keeping price growth in check," said Redfin Senior Economist Asad Khan.

Affordability Gains Fail to Boost Pending Home Sales

Despite incremental improvements in affordability, pending home sales in January dropped by 0.8% to an all-time low going back to 2001, building on a 7.4% decline in December, according to the National Association of Realtors (NAR). NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun noted that potential homebuyers could drive up home prices unless more supply becomes available.

In recent months, President Trump has issued several measures in an attempt to improve affordability, including an executive order barring large institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes and calling on Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae (FNMA) to purchase $200 million of mortgage-backed securities.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 2/19/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading lower on Thursday as investors digest a trade balance update and fresh comments from President Trump on Iran.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Despite the average U.S. import tariff rate rising to 13% from 2.6% over the course of 2025, the trade deficit for the year only slightly narrowed to $901 billion from $904 billion in 2024 and remains near an all-time high. In addition, the deficit for December increased to $70.3 billion from $56.8 billion, above the consensus estimate of $55.5 billion.

Trump Ramps Up Pressure Against Iran

Furthermore, Trump has amped up the pressure on Iran to scrap its nuclear program, saying he would give the country around 10 days to reach a deal or face U.S. military action.

"Good talks are being had. It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

In recent days, the U.S. has bolstered its military presence in the Middle East as talks between the two sides continue.

data.title: Trump Warns 'Bad Things' Will Happen to Iran Without a Nuclear Deal
data.description:
President Trump has warned that Iran will face "bad things" if it doesn't agree to a deal that would halt its nuclear program, adding that it would be around 10 days before either a deal is made or the U.S. launches an attack against the country.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Good talks are being had. It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C., on Thursday. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

Congress Moves to Limit Strikes on Iran

The two sides met earlier this week to discuss terms, with both the U.S. and Iran saying that progress had been made. At the same time, the U.S. has increased its military presence in the Middle East, while Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has posted aggressive statements on X, including a warning that the U.S. military could be "struck so hard that it cannot get up again."

Several members of Congress have voiced their opposition to attacking Iran. California Democrat Ro Khanna and Kentucky Republican Thomas Massie have planned a war powers resolution vote for next week that would limit Trump's ability to strike Iran without congressional approval.

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Drops as Trade Deficit Widens Sharply; Trump Sends Warning to Iran
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Thursday as trade and geopolitical concerns rain on a market rebound.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. trade deficit widened to $70.3 billion in December from $56.8 billion during the previous month as growth in imports outpaced exports. Imports increased by 3.6% to $357.6 billion, while exports fell by 1.7% to $287.3 billion. Rising imports negatively impact gross domestic product (GDP) because they expand the trade deficit by sending spending overseas.

Furthermore, the trade deficit for 2025 only slightly improved to $901 billion from $904 billion in 2024 and remains near historically high levels. When excluding services, the goods deficit increased by 2.1% to an all-time high of $1.24 trillion. That comes despite higher import tariffs and a call to revive domestic production from the Trump administration.

Meanwhile, President Trump urged Iran to halt its nuclear program, warning that "bad things" would happen if the country fails to reach a deal with the U.S.

"It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

Elsewhere, employers remain hesitant to lay off workers, with initial jobless claims falling by 23,000 to 206,000 for the week ended February 14. The claims are at their lowest level in five weeks, while the weekly drop of 23,000 was the largest since November.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.52% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Kashkari Slams Hassett over Critical Fed Tariff Study Comments
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari criticized recent comments from National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett casting doubt on the accuracy of a New York Fed tariff study, labeling it as another attempt to undermine the Fed's independence. The study noted that U.S. businesses and consumers have absorbed nearly 90% of the costs of President Trump's tariffs.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"This is just another step to try to compromise the Fed's independence," said Kashkari on Thursday. "Over the last year we've seen multiple attempts to try to compromise the Fed's independence."

Kashkari Dismisses 'Noise,' Reaffirms Fed Goals

Earlier this week, Hassett characterized the study as an "embarrassment" and suggested that the authors should be "disciplined."

Kashkari added that the Justice Department's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell is another example of Fed independence at risk. He reiterated that the central bank will remain steadfast in its dual mandate of maximizing employment and keeping inflation contained while tuning out "noise."

data.title: Nvidia or Microsoft: Ken Griffin Bets Big on One Top AI Stock
data.description:
AI-led disruption is real, but any claims the tech is about to devour all it encounters should probably be taken with a grain of salt. That, in any case, is the opinion of billionaire Ken Griffin, who recently argued that the AI boom is being driven as much by narrative as by tangible productivity improvements.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Griffin, who has a net worth of about $49.5 billion and is the founder, CEO and Co-Chief Investment Officer of hedge fund Citadel, added that this take is not intended to diminish AI's power, but he believes there's plenty of hype attached to AI, and it serves to rationalize massive levels of spending.

The hyperscalers are projected to spend nearly $700 billion this year on AI-related capital expenditure. Is it hype? "Of course," Ken Griffin said, citing the unprecedented amount of capital now flowing into AI infrastructure. "You're not going to generate this kind of spend unless you're going to make a promise you're going to profoundly change the world."

Yet, despite the skepticism, Griffin isn't sitting on the sidelines. AI stocks make their presence felt in the Citadel portfolio, and recently Griffin has been reshuffling his holdings of AI stalwarts Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), betting big on one name while reducing his exposure to the other. So, let's find out what's behind the investing heavyweight's latest moves.

Nvidia

The days when Nvidia was primarily a gaming-focused company now feel like a distant era, even though it wasn't that long ago that gaming still made up the majority of its revenue. It was only from 2022 onwards that the data center segment became the primary driver of revenue, fueled, of course, by growing AI demand.

As it turned out, the gaming GPUs Nvidia pioneered were ideally suited for AI. Their design for highly parallel graphics processing made them ideal for the large-scale calculations AI models need. Nvidia's years of optimizing performance and memory for gaming gave it a head start, helping the company become a major enabler of the AI boom.

That head start made its chips the industry standard, but Nvidia is more than just hardware. CUDA, its proprietary parallel computing platform, has been a key differentiator. Because developers build AI models and applications specifically optimized for CUDA, researchers, hyperscalers, and enterprises become embedded in Nvidia's software stack. That ecosystem effect makes Nvidia far more influential than a typical chipmaker.

Famously, the uniqueness and excellence of its offerings have turned Nvidia into the world's most valuable company, although in recent times, the stock's performance has been more muted, as the market has mulled over the possibility of overheated valuations and an AI bubble.

While Griffin has called out the hype around AI, he evidently does not think it applies to Nvidia. During Q4, Citadel bought 11,739,611 NVDA shares, upping its stake by 120%, a position currently worth $2.2 billion.

That move will make sense to Cantor's C.J. Muse, an analyst ranked in 11th spot among the thousands of Wall Street stock experts, who thinks the stock is primed to push higher over the coming months.

"We are still in the very beginning of the next industrial revolution driven by proliferation of Agentic + Physical AI (likely driving the largest buildout in history)," the 5-star analyst said. "So the setup is extremely positive, particularly when we reflect on shares only trading 21x/16x expected CY26/27 earnings… Our sense is that this earnings report will mark the beginning of an inflection point for shares, followed by the latest frontier models built on Blackwell released in March highlighting NVDA's technological leadership, GTC San Jose in March further highlighting the company's leadership in AI, and finally a likely Data Center revenue guide update for CY27 from Jensen as early as Computex in early June given our vision for AI lead times to continue to extend given the insatiable demand for AI Accelerated Compute."

With that backdrop, Muse rates NVDA shares Overweight (i.e., Buy), and his $300 price target implies 59% upside over the next 12 months. (To watch Muse's track record, click here)

Overall, aside from just one Hold and one Sell, all 37 other recent analyst reviews are positive, giving the stock a Strong Buy consensus rating. Even the more measured $261.84 average price target suggests shares could climb 39% over the coming year. (See Nvidia stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: XRP Price Gets a 'Real Honest Take' from Analyst Who Says 'Capital Isn't Ready to Be Fully Deployed Yet'
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) currently sits between extreme optimism and harsh reality. While some fans still dream of $100 prices, the actual market data shows a coin struggling to find its footing after falling far from its July 2025 high of $3.66. Market analyst Zach Humphries recently offered a reality check, describing the current situation as the "real honest take" on where the token is headed. Regarding the disconnect between the project's potential and its current price, Humphries noted that "The story is strong, the narrative's good, but the capital isn't ready to be fully deployed yet" as he explained why the market is currently stalled.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Global Economics Drive the Market Slump

The crypto market doesn't live on an island. Zach Humphries pointed out that outside forces like government policies and the upcoming change in the US Federal Reserve leadership are making investors nervous. Skepticism toward risk assets like XRP is growing as people worry about interest rates and trade tensions. According to Humphries, these macroeconomic factors are the main reason XRP and other coins have been seeing heavy red candles on the charts since the market topped out last October.

Realistic Math Crushes the $100 Dream

One of the most common myths in the XRP community is that the coin can reach triple digits regardless of its market cap. Humphries was quick to shut this down, stating that valuation really does matter and that massive assets simply don't make those kinds of jumps without a perfect economic environment. He believes the market has not yet reached its true bottom. Based on historical cycles where it takes about 12 months to hit the floor after a peak, we might not see the final price bottom until late 2026.

New Liquidity Drives the Next Rally

While XRP has made great strides in legal clarity and new partnerships, it still needs a spark to move higher. Humphries identified liquidity as the single most important catalyst for the next bullish development. He explained that "XRP trades largely on, you know, the general crypto sentiment, and more than anything, liquidity" during his analysis. Without new money flowing in from the broader financial system, XRP will likely lack the power to sustain a real climb. He suggests that things like institutional treasury allocations and more XRP ETFs will be the fuel the coin needs. Until then, he advises holders to stay calm and use a DCA strategy to weather the storm.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4090.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: United Airlines Stock (UAL) Hits Turbulence on Plan to Cut Travel Rewards
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) stock is down 4% in early trading on Feb. 19 after the carrier announced changes to its travel rewards.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Management at Chicago-based United said that they are revamping their MileagePlus frequent flyer program to reward travelers that use the airline's credit cards. Going forward, United will reward travelers with more miles and lower redemption rates, including for some of its long-haul business-class seats, provided they also use the company's credit cards.

United said the changes are being made to reward its highest-spending customers. The shift marks the biggest shake-up to United Airlines' rewards program in more than a decade, when the carrier began rewarding customers for how much they spent rather than for how far they flew. Investors have been quick to give the latest change a thumbs down, pushing UAL stock lower.

The changes take effect April 2.

United Airlines' Credit Cards

The changes to United's loyalty program are part of a wider trend among airlines to reward frequent flyer program members for how much money they spend. About a decade ago, the major carriers tweaked their loyalty programs to reward customers for dollars spent rather than miles traveled.

United is encouraging customers to sign up for its credit cards by offering additional perks like no fees for checked bags and earlier boarding. Going forward, United MileagePlus credit card holders will get more miles per dollar spent on United flights compared with customers without the airline's card. United will also now allow customers with one of its credit cards to redeem their miles for flights at a 10% discount.

United Airlines partners exclusively with JPMorgan Chase (JPM) to issue its co-branded credit cards in the U.S.

Is UAL Stock a Buy?

United Airlines' stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UAL price target of $142.32 implies 25.67% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
